openQuestions.text: Q1: snscrape status — is it still maintained in 2026? Rate limits? Alternatives (Apify, Nitter, official APIs)? Multi-platform support (TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, Twitter/X)?
Q2: pytrends / Google Trends — rate limits, real-time vs daily trends, geo-filtering (Thailand), category filtering, rising vs top queries, related topics?
Q3: TikTok Trends discovery — official Research API, Creative Center trends API, TikTok Discover page scraping, trending hashtags/sounds, rate limits?
Q4: YouTube trending — Data API v3 `videos.list(chart=mostPopular)`, category filtering, geo-filtering (TH), quota cost, trending vs search volume?
Q5: BERTopic configuration — Thai+English mixed text handling, optimal parameters, online/incremental learning for real-time trends, topic representation, visualization?
Q6: Trend freshness & velocity — how to measure trend momentum (rising/peaking/declining), timing window for content creation, trend lifecycle modeling?
Q7: LLM-as-judge scoring rubric — 6 dimensions (hook, storytelling, emotion, visual, audio, CTA), exact prompt design, scoring scale, calibration methodology?
Q8: Hook variant generation — how to generate multiple hook variants from a single trend, template system, A/B testing integration with Content Lab?
Q9: GBDT model training — feature engineering from 500+ videos, LightGBM vs XGBoost comparison, training pipeline design, what metrics to predict (views? engagement rate?)?
Q10: Scoring calibration & feedback loop — how to validate LLM scores against actual performance (L7 feedback), score drift detection, retraining triggers?
Q11: Thai-specific considerations — Thai NLP for trend text (PyThaiNLP), Thai social media patterns, Thai viral content characteristics, Thai slang/internet culture trends?
Q12: n8n orchestration — how L1 triggers L2, how L2 triggers L3 Content Lab, cron schedules, workflow chaining, data flow through DB tables (trends, content)?

keyFindings.text: **Assessment**: BEST data source for viral-ops. Official, reliable, generous quota, Thai region support.
**Assessment**: Creative Center (free, no auth) + Apify scraper is the pragmatic path. Research API for academic only. Official Developer APIs require app approval.
**Assessment**: pytrends is VIABLE. Core tool for L1. Consider pytrends-modern for production robustness.
**Assessment**: snscrape is NOT viable for viral-ops. Twitter/X broken, TikTok/YouTube never supported. Must use alternatives.
**Cost efficiency**: At 1 unit/call with 50 results each, you can fetch 500,000 trending videos per day within free quota
**Critical**: Twitter/X scraping broken since June 2023 (Issue #783, #996) -- Twitter locked behind login wall, no known workaround
**Endpoint**: `videos.list(chart='mostPopular', regionCode='TH')`
**Geo-filtering**: Fully supported. Thailand = `geo='TH'`. Sub-regions available (e.g., `TH-10` for Bangkok).
**Google Trends official API**: Launched 2025, still in alpha with limited endpoints and quotas. 1,500/day quota.

**Issue #1037** ("Question about the future of snscrape") -- user reports project "has been lasting for a few months" in broken state; maintainer response not visible but community sentiment is frustration
**Key methods**: `interest_over_time()`, `interest_by_region()`, `related_topics()`, `related_queries()`, `trending_searches()`, `suggestions()`
**maxResults**: 1-50 per request (default 5)
**Modern alternative**: `pytrends-modern` (v0.2.5) -- enhanced error handling, automatic retries, rate limit management, proxy rotation, smart backoff
**No deprecation**: Endpoint fully supported, no deprecation notices
**NOT listed**: TikTok, YouTube -- snscrape never supported these platforms
**Python requirement**: 3.8+ (outdated minimum suggests stale maintenance)
**Quota**: 10,000 units/day free. `videos.list` costs only 1 unit per call. Compare: `search.list` costs 100 units.
**Quota increase**: Can apply for higher quota through Google Cloud Console
**Rate limits**: No official limit documented. Practical limit ~1,400 sequential requests before throttle. Google blocks excessive requests. Recommended: 60s sleep between requests when rate-limited.
**Real-time trends**: `trending_searches(pn='thailand')` for real-time; `realtime_trending_searches()` for live trends
**regionCode**: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2. Thailand = `TH`. Works only with `chart` parameter.
**Repository**: github.com/GeneralMills/pytrends -- actively maintained, latest PyPI version 4.1.0
**Repository**: github.com/JustAnotherArchivist/snscrape -- 5.3k stars, 776 forks, NOT archived but functionally unmaintained
**Response parts**: `snippet` (title, description, tags, thumbnails), `statistics` (views, likes, comments), `contentDetails` (duration, definition), `topicDetails` (topic categories)
**Rising vs Top**: `related_queries()` returns both "top" (highest volume) and "rising" (fastest growing) -- critical for trend velocity detection
**Scrapfly guide**: Documents unofficial API endpoints for TikTok trend scraping
[SOURCE: https://apify.com/doliz/tiktok-creative-center-scraper/api]

[SOURCE: https://www.getphyllo.com/post/youtube-api-limits-how-to-calculate-api-usage-cost-and-fix-exceeded-api-quota]
**Supported platforms listed**: Facebook, Instagram, Mastodon, Reddit, Telegram, Twitter, VKontakte, Weibo
**Third-party scraping**: Apify has a "TikTok Creative Center Scraper" actor for programmatic access to Creative Center data
**TickerTrends**: Third-party API providing TikTok hashtag analytics and time-series data

**TikTok Creative Center**: Free, no login required for most features. Shows trending hashtags, sounds, creators, videos. Filterable by region and time (7-30 days). This is the primary trend discovery surface.
**TikTok Developer Portal** (developers.tiktok.com): Offers Hashtag Analytics API, Trending Content API, Display API. Requires app registration.
**TikTok Research API**: Academic/research only. Must submit research plan for approval. Strict data use limits. NOT suitable for commercial trend discovery.
**videoCategoryId**: Filter by category (default=0 for all). Categories available via `videoCategories.list`.
**Video Query API**: Available at `developers.tiktok.com/doc/research-api-specs-query-videos/` for querying video data programmatically
`gprop='youtube'` can provide YouTube-specific trend data through Google Trends (complementing YouTube Data API)
`IncrementalPCA(n_components=5)` replaces UMAP for dimensionality reduction
`interest_by_region(resolution)` -- geographic breakdown
`interest_over_time()` -- time-series volume data for trend velocity measurement
`MiniBatchKMeans(n_clusters=50)` replaces HDBSCAN for clustering
`multilingual-e5-large` (mE5) -- higher quality for cross-lingual semantic similarity but heavier (1024 dims)
`'now 1-H'` -- last hour (real-time)
`'now 4-H'` -- last 4 hours
`OnlineCountVectorizer(decay=0.01)` handles vocabulary growth with time-weighted decay
`paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2` -- BERTopic's built-in multilingual default (50+ languages, 384 dims). Fastest option.
`realtime_trending_searches(pn, cat, count)` -- real-time trends with story summaries
`related_queries()` -- returns BOTH `rising` and `top` related terms (critical for trend expansion)
`related_topics()` -- related topics with rising/top split
`'today 1-m'` -- last 30 days
`'today 3-m'` -- last 90 days
`'today 5-y'` -- last 5 years (default)
`today_searches(pn)` -- today's trending searches
`trending_searches(pn)` -- hot daily searches by region
`WangchanBERTa` (`airesearch/wangchanberta-base-att-spm-uncased`) -- Thai-specific, pre-trained on 78GB Thai text. Best for Thai-only content but DOES NOT handle English well.
Authentication/cookie management against TikTok Creative Center
**Categories supported**: All 5 trending types
For n8n cron: poll `trending_searches(pn='thailand')` every 2 hours (matching gen1 architecture)
**Industry**: category dropdown for vertical filtering
**Input schema**: Not fully documented on the API page -- likely accepts region, trending type, and period as parameters
**Limitation**: Detailed input/output field schemas not exposed on the public API docs page; requires running a test execution to see full output structure
**No official rate limit number exists** -- empirical reports suggest ~10-20 requests/minute is safe without proxies
**Output**: Structured JSON data
**Pagination**: 20 items per page with "View More" button
Pre-trained embedding model (no updates needed -- SentenceTransformer stays frozen)
**Pricing**: Pay-per-event model with free trial available
**Region**: dropdown with "All regions" default (Thailand available)
Region filtering (Thailand)
Structured JSON output ready for n8n webhook ingestion
The archived status of pytrends means `pytrends-modern` (with proxy rotation via `proxy_list` and cookie refresh) should be used for production
**Time period**: URL parameter `period=7` confirmed (7-day window), likely also 30-day and 120-day options
**Top Ads** -- best-performing ad creatives by region/vertical
**Trending Creators** -- creator profiles with engagement metrics
**Trending Hashtags** -- name, post count (e.g., "337K Posts"), industry category, rank position, rank change (up/down arrows), "New to top 100" badge
**Trending Songs/Sounds** -- sound usage counts, associated videos
**Trending Videos** -- captions, hashtags, creators, sounds, metrics, timestamps
Use `backoff_factor` parameter in pytrends constructor for automatic retry
Use `interest_over_time()` as a secondary signal to measure trend velocity (rising vs declining)

**1-5 categorical integer scale** is strongly preferred. Float scores produce inconsistent results. "LLM-as-judge does better with a categorical integer scoring scale with a very clear explanation" (Monte Carlo Data, 2025).
**Decay** (velocity < -0.05, strength > 30) -- declining interest. Content already produced may still perform but do not invest new production.
**Emergence** (velocity > +0.20, strength < 30) -- weak signal, fast growth. Scout phase.
**Few-shot calibration**: Include 1-2 examples maximum. Research shows performance declines with 3+ examples.
**Growth** (velocity > +0.20, strength 30-70) -- gaining momentum. Best window for content creation.
**Peak** (velocity -0.05 to +0.05, strength > 70) -- high volume, flat momentum. Last viable production window.
**Saturation** (velocity < -0.20, strength < 30) -- trend is over. Archive.
**Separate evaluation per dimension** -- LLMs are "much more effective when given clear, single objective tasks." Avoid combining all 6 dimensions into one mega-prompt.
**Step decomposition**: Break subjective assessments into reasoning steps before scoring.
After T+168h, compare variant performance:
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Binary viral threshold**: Loses too much information. Regression is more useful than classification here.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Celebrity amplification**: Thai celebrity/KOL sharing dramatically accelerates trends. A single share from a top creator can push a trend from emergence to peak in <12h.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Content Lab produces 2-3 hook variants per topic
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Content preferences**: Comedy/humor, food, beauty/skincare, travel, and relationship content consistently top Thai trending lists. "React" content (reacting to trends) is uniquely popular in Thailand.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Cultural humor patterns**: Slapstick, situational comedy, self-deprecating humor, and "แกล้ง" (pranking) content have disproportionately high viral potential.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Differential analysis: which feature dimensions differ most between winner/loser
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Each variant gets a separate `video_id` in L7 analytics
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Emotional triggers**: "สงสาร" (sympathy/pity), "ฮา" (humor), and "ดราม่า" (drama) are the three strongest emotional triggers for Thai audiences.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Engagement rate**: Secondary target. Views are more directly controllable and predictable by content quality
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Feed hook_category performance statistics back as `similar_hook_category_performance` feature
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: For cold-start phase (<500 videos), use XGBoost with `max_depth=3-4` to prevent overfitting on small data. Switch to LightGBM once dataset exceeds 500 rows.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Hashtag parsing**: Thai hashtags are unsegmented text (e.g., #ไม่กินก็ไม่ตาย). Must run word segmentation on hashtag text after stripping `#` prefix to extract meaningful terms for BERTopic clustering.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: LightGBM's 20x speed advantage matters for the feedback loop -- retraining on new L7 data should be fast.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: LightGBM's native categorical feature handling is critical because 4 features are categorical (trend_lifecycle_stage, hook_category, niche_id, platform_target). One-hot encoding these for XGBoost would expand feature space unnecessarily.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Losers' features become negative examples
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Named Entity Recognition**: `pythainlp.tag.NER` for extracting brand names, celebrity names, locations from trend text.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Peak hours**: Thai content peaks at 19:00-22:00 ICT (UTC+7) weekdays, 12:00-14:00 on weekends. Late-night (22:00-01:00) has a secondary peak for entertainment/humor content.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Phase 0 fallback**: Use the composite_viral_score formula from iter-3 as the predictor (weights: content_quality 0.40, trend_freshness 0.35, niche_fit 0.15, timing 0.10)
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Platform dominance**: TikTok and YouTube Shorts are the primary short-form video platforms in Thailand. LINE (messaging) is dominant for sharing but not for discovery. Facebook remains large for longer content.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Raw views**: Biased by follower count -- a 100K-follower account getting 50K views is underperforming while a 1K-follower account getting 50K views is viral
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Sentiment analysis**: Pre-labeled social media dataset included (positive/neutral/negative/question) -- useful for emotional trigger dimension in L2 scoring.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Social media domain**: PyThaiNLP includes `han_solo` CRF syllable segmenter specifically tuned for Thai social media text -- handles slang, abbreviations, and code-switching better than standard engines.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Speed**: Thai trends move FAST. Typical lifecycle: 24-48h from emergence to peak (vs 72-120h for global English trends). The 48-72h content production window from iter-3 may need tightening to 24-36h for Thai market.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Synthetic bootstrapping**: Scrape competitor videos with public metrics + apply LLM-as-judge scoring to their content. Builds training data without publishing own videos.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: T+168h provides stable measurement (algorithmic distribution mostly settled by day 7)
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Time-to-X-views**: Too noisy, depends heavily on posting time and algorithmic push timing
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Transfer learning**: If available, use public TikTok/YouTube analytics datasets to pre-train on general engagement features, then fine-tune on own data.
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Winner's features become positive training examples
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: **Word segmentation**: Critical for Thai (no spaces between words). `pythainlp.tokenize.word_tokenize(text, engine='newmm')` for dictionary-based fast segmentation, or `engine='deepcut'` for neural-based (higher accuracy on social media text, slower).
sources: https://peerj.com/articles/cs-3245.pdf -- PeerJ 2025 social media topic popularity ML study

keyFindings.text: Always include error notification nodes in scheduled workflows
sources: https://apify.com/doliz/tiktok-creative-center-scraper -- Full input/output schema for TikTok CC scraper

keyFindings.text: Asynchronous -- parent does not wait for completion
sources: https://apify.com/doliz/tiktok-creative-center-scraper -- Full input/output schema for TikTok CC scraper

keyFindings.text: BERTopic + sentence-transformers requires ~2GB RAM and GPU-optional compute

keyFindings.text: Child receives items from parent, processes them, returns results back to parent

ruledOutDirections.text: **snscrape as multi-platform scraper for viral-ops**: Definitively eliminated. The platforms we need (TikTok, YouTube) were never supported, and its Twitter support is broken. Should be removed from the architecture.
**snscrape for TikTok/YouTube**: Never supported these platforms at all. Gen1 mention was misleading -- snscrape cannot scrape TikTok or YouTube.
**snscrape for Twitter/X**: Broken since June 2023, no fix available. Platform locked behind login wall.
**TikTok Research API for commercial use**: Academic-only, requires research plan approval, strict data use policies.
**pytrends (original) for production**: Repository archived April 2025. Use `pytrends-modern` fork for maintained proxy rotation and cookie handling.
**WangchanBERTa as primary BERTopic embedding**: Thai-only model, cannot handle the English+Thai mixed text common in Thai social media trends. Use multilingual model instead.

**WangchanBERTa for mixed-language trend clustering**: Definitively unsuitable -- it's a Thai-only RoBERTa variant that will produce garbage embeddings for English tokens. Not a "try harder" situation; the model architecture lacks English vocabulary.

**Float scoring scales for LLM-as-judge**: Research strongly favors categorical integers (1-5). Float scores produce inconsistent, harder-to-calibrate results. Ruled out 0-10 scale from gen1 in favor of 1-5.
None this iteration. All approaches were productive.
**Single mega-prompt for all 6 dimensions**: LLMs perform better with single-objective tasks. Multi-dimension prompts lead to criterion conflation and halo effects.
**Binary viral classification for GBDT**: Loses too much information versus regression. A continuous prediction (log-normalized) provides richer signal for ranking content ideas.
**Random train/test split for temporal data**: Would create data leakage from future trends appearing in training data. Temporal split is mandatory.
**Raw view count as GBDT target**: Biased by follower count. Normalized `views/followers` is required for meaningful comparison across accounts of different sizes.
**Standard dictionary-based segmentation for Thai social media**: Too many OOV (out-of-vocabulary) failures on slang. Neural or CRF-based models (deepcut, han_solo) required.
**Real-time webhook from data sources to L2**: Data sources (pytrends, Apify, YouTube API) do not support push/webhook notifications. Polling is the only option.
**Single monolithic n8n workflow for L1+L2**: Too complex for debugging, n8n performance degrades with 50+ nodes. Sub-workflow pattern is mandatory.

**tiktok-discover-api.vercel.app as production source**: The self-hosted API returned 404 on multiple endpoint patterns during this iteration. The service appears unreliable. Use Apify actor as primary TikTok CC source.

**tiktok-discover-api.vercel.app endpoints**: Tried `/api/getTrendingHastag?region=TH` and `/api/trending/hashtag?region=TH` -- both returned 404. The free unofficial API appears down or has changed its URL structure without documentation update. Not viable for production use.
